title: AI is not just about training anymore. It is about inference. Enterprise ...

AI is not just about training anymore. It is about inference. ⚡
Enterprise focus is shifting toward real time AI, with the inference market projected to reach up to $137B by 2030 and hybrid and edge deployments rapidly gaining ground.
The takeaway? Infrastructure decisions made today will define AI outcomes tomorrow.
See link in bio for more.
bibissimoura
1w
Why is impossible to reach contact to support???? I'm trying to solve a problem and nobody answers!!!! I bought a loq essential with 1Tb and you guys sent me half of it!!!!! This is completely absurd!!!!
Like Reply
derbent_1971
2w
Strategic Imperative: Eliminating the Memory Bottleneck in Rubin R100 via LPDDR6Dear Nvidia Leadership Team,The second half of 2026 marks the arrival of hardware built natively for AI. The mobile ecosystem has already set the baseline: next-generation flagship chips—including the Apple A20 Pro, Qualcomm Snapdragon 8 Elite Gen 6 Pro, MediaTek Dimensity 9700, and Samsung Exynos 2700—are all adopting LPDDR6 as their native, specialized AI memory.To maintain absolute dominance, Nvidia must match this standard. Paired with the Vera CPU, the upcoming Nvidia Rubin R100 must be sold exclusively with LPDDR6 RAM.Integrating legacy LPDDR5X into the Rubin platform is an unacceptable compromise. LPDDR5X was never originally engineered for AI workloads. Using it creates a severe bottleneck, forcing a revolutionary AI architecture to rely on a legacy standard. It makes no strategic sense for premium enterprise AI hardware to use memory inferior to what is found in standard consumer smartphones.The Rubin R100 and Vera CPU platform will only become a fully realized, zero-compromise AI solution when paired with native, AI-centric LPDDR6 memory to perfectly complement the HBM4 array. We strongly urge Nvidia to refuse legacy standards and mandate LPDDR6 for the Vera-Rubin ecosystem. Do not let memory become the weak link in the next generation of AI.
